info.name: E-Credentialing CVO Platform — Public REST API v1 (v1.6.0)
info.description:
The v1 surface is intentionally read-only. Every request requires
a Bearer API key with the appropriate scope; every request is
audited; PHI fields (SSN, DOB, DEA, personal address, notes) are
NEVER returned regardless of scope.

For a no-auth playground using synthetic data, see
`/api/sandbox/v1/*` (documented at `/sandbox`).

Rate limiting (since v1.2.0): every successful response carries
`X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and
`X-RateLimit-Reset` headers (Unix-seconds). When you exceed the
per-key budget the API returns a `429` with the `RateLimitProblem`
body and a `Retry-After` header.

Request correlation (since v1.3.0): every request and response —
success or error — carries an `X-Request-Id` header. Send your
own (`^[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{8,128}$`) and we'll honour it; otherwise
we generate one server-side. Paste it into a support ticket and
we can find your call in O(1). See `docs/api/versioning.md`
for the full contract and deprecation policy.

Key introspection (since v1.4.0): `GET /api/v1/me` returns the
current API key's name, granted scopes, lifecycle timestamps,
and current rate-limit budget. Pairs with `/health` to make
"is my key configured correctly?" a one-call answer.

Pagination links (since v1.5.0): every paginated list response
carries an RFC 8288 `Link` header listing `first`, `prev`,
`next`, and `last` page URLs. Clients can navigate the result
set without parsing the JSON envelope.

Conditional GETs (since v1.6.0): every read endpoint emits a
weak `ETag` response header derived from a stable hash of the
cacheable subset of the response body. Send the value back as
`If-None-Match: "<etag>"` on the next poll; if nothing has
changed the API replies `304 Not Modified` with an empty body
(zero JSON bytes). Saves bandwidth, DB round-trips, and your
rate-limit budget. The 304 still carries `X-Request-Id` and
the rate-limit headers.

Wave 8 (2026-04-18). See ADR 0020 and `docs/qa/per-pillar/pillar-j-api.md`.

Auto-generated from `docs/api/openapi-v1.yaml`.
Do not hand-edit — regenerate via `npm run postman:gen`.

variable.description: Base URL of your environment, no trailing slash.

query.description: Case-insensitive substring filter on payer name.

request.description:
Verifies that the supplied API key is active and that the
environment is reachable. Returns the keyId so the caller can
confirm which key is in use without echoing the secret, the
API surface version, and a server-side timestamp for
clock-skew checks.

Authentication: ANY active API key. Unlike every other v1
operation, this endpoint does NOT require a specific scope
— a brand-new, scopeless key returns 200 here. This makes
it the natural first call when wiring up the SDK.

Versioning: shipped in v1.1.0 (SemVer minor — additive
only). See `docs/api/versioning.md`.

Returns the current API key's name, granted scopes,
creation/expiration/last-used timestamps, and the current
rate-limit budget snapshot. The canonical "what can my key
actually do?" check.

Authentication: ANY active API key. Like `/health`, no
specific scope is required - a brand-new, scopeless key
returns 200 here. This makes it useful for debugging "why
am I getting 403?" issues without a chicken-and-egg scope
requirement.

Privacy: never returns the bearer key, the key hash, or
any PHI. The `name` field is the human-readable label set
by the admin who created the key (e.g.
"Production prod-east"); it is safe to surface to the end
user as "you are authenticated as X".

Versioning: shipped in v1.4.0 (SemVer minor - additive
only). See `docs/api/versioning.md`.
Get one provider (full envelope, no PHI)

Streams a fully-formed Curriculum Vitae as `application/pdf`.
Requires the `providers:cv` scope. The CV includes only data
commonly published in CVs (name, NPI, provider type, education,
licensure, board certs, hospital privileges, work history,
CME log) — never SSN, DOB, DEA, or personal addresses.

Returns a paginated list of providers. Supports filtering by
`status` and `npi`. PHI is never included.
